response:
Sub-problem 1: The optimal schedule orders scenes by decade to avoid consecutive same-decade films. Movies from 1994 (4 days), 2004 (6 days), 2007 (8 days), 2012 (10 days), and 2015 (12 days) must be sequenced. A valid order is 1994 (4 days), 2004 (6 days), 2012 (10 days), 2007 (8 days), 2015 (12 days). This sequence alternates decades: 90s, 00s, 10s, 00s, 10s. The total shooting days remain the sum of all individual days: 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 + 12 = 40 days. No reduction in total days is possible as all scenes must be shot, so the minimum total is 40 days.

******
Scene one costs four lakhs-Scene two costs nine lakhs-Scene three costs sixteen lakhs-Scene four costs twenty five lakhs-Scene five costs thirty six lakhs-Sum of first four is fifty four lakhs-Only three scenes fit within budget-Answer is three scenes completed.
